goal: Land the foundational `ort` (ONNX Runtime) integration in `crates/ucil-embeddings/` per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786 ('ucil-embeddings crate: ONNX Runtime (`ort` crate) inference'). This WO supersedes the abandoned WO-0054 (no branch ever pushed; no progress on main; the only artifact is the work-order JSON itself committed at `92e76a3`). The CRITICAL CORRECTION over WO-0054: the frozen acceptance-test selector in `feature-list.json` is `-p ucil-embeddings onnx_inference::` (NOT `ort_session::`), so the NEW module file MUST be named `onnx_inference.rs`. Create `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs` (NEW module) declaring `pub struct OnnxSession` whose `OnnxSession::from_path(model_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, OnnxSessionError>` opens an ONNX model file via `ort::Session::builder().commit_from_file(...)` and exposes `input_names()` / `output_names()` accessors plus `infer(token_ids: &[i64]) -> Result<Vec<f32>, OnnxSessionError>` per the feature description ('infer() accepts token IDs and returns embedding vectors'). The frozen acceptance test `onnx_inference::test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model` lands at MODULE ROOT of `onnx_inference.rs` per DEC-0007, exercising session creation + metadata + a single infer round-trip against a tiny synthetic ONNX model checked in at `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/minimal.onnx`. This WO is foundational for P2-W8-F02 (CodeRankEmbed model load), P2-W8-F03 (Qwen3 GPU upgrade), P2-W8-F05 (chunker), P2-W8-F06 (throughput bench) — all those features consume `OnnxSession`.
plan_summary: Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs` (~140-220 LOC including module-level `//!` doc + `OnnxSessionError` + `OnnxSession` impl + 1 frozen test). Add `ort = { version = "2", default-features = false, features = ["std", "download-binaries"] }` and `ndarray = { version = "0.16", default-features = false, features = ["std"] }` to workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion). Add `ort.workspace = true` and `ndarray.workspace = true` to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`. The `OnnxSession` struct stores an `ort::Session` plus cached `Vec<String>` of input/output names so callers can introspect without re-querying the session each call. `OnnxSession::from_path` does (1) `Session::builder()?.commit_from_file(model_path)` (synchronous I/O — `ort` is sync-by-default; future async wrap via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` is the natural F02 follow-up), (2) `let input_names = session.inputs.iter().map(|i| i.name.clone()).collect();` (3) `let output_names = session.outputs.iter().map(|o| o.name.clone()).collect();` (4) `Ok(Self { session, input_names, output_names })`. `OnnxSession::infer(token_ids: &[i64])` does (1) build a 2-D `ndarray::Array2<i64>` of shape `[1, token_ids.len()]` (single-batch row vector), (2) wrap as `ort::Value` via `ort::inputs!{ self.input_names[0].as_str() => array.view() }?`, (3) `let outputs = self.session.run(inputs)?;`, (4) extract first output as `outputs[0].try_extract_tensor::<f32>()?`, (5) flatten to `Vec<f32>` and return. `OnnxSessionError` is a `thiserror::Error` enum with `#[non_exhaustive]` and variants `Ort { source: ort::Error }`, `Ndarray { source: ndarray::ShapeError }`, `Io { source: std::io::Error }`, `MissingInput { name: String }`, `MissingOutput { name: String }`. The test `test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model` is `#[test]` (sync — `ort` API is sync) at module root of `onnx_inference.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Test exercises 4 sub-assertions: (SA1) `OnnxSession::from_path` succeeds against the bundled minimal model; (SA2) `input_names().len() >= 1` and the first input name matches the minimal model's known input (e.g. `"input_ids"`); (SA3) `output_names().len() >= 1`; (SA4) `infer(&[1i64, 2, 3])` returns `Vec<f32>` with the expected length per the minimal model's output shape. Add `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/minimal.onnx` — a tiny synthetic ONNX model (~200-2000 bytes) whose graph is a single Identity / Cast op consuming `input_ids: int64[1, *]` and producing `embedding: float32[1, *]` (or similar shape that lets `infer(&[i64; 3])` succeed). The executor MAY generate this via a one-shot `python3 -c "import onnx; ..."` helper at WO setup (committed alongside the .onnx fixture for reproducibility), OR copy a public-domain tiny ONNX model from upstream `ort` test fixtures with attribution. Add `pub mod onnx_inference;` to `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs` and a re-export line `pub use onnx_inference::{OnnxSession, OnnxSessionError};` per the WO-0042 → WO-0056 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 12 consecutive cleared). Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh`. Pre-bake 2 mutation entries (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons): (M1) `from_path` body neutered to early `Err(OnnxSessionError::MissingInput { name: String::new() })` → SA1 panics on `OnnxSession::from_path` returning Err; (M2) `infer` body neutered to early `Ok(Vec::new())` → SA4 panics on empty Vec when expected length > 0.
scope_in: Add to `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` section, alphabetical positions: `ndarray = { version = "0.16", default-features = false, features = ["std"] }` and `ort = { version = "2", default-features = false, features = ["std", "download-binaries"] }`. The `download-binaries` feature pulls a pre-built ONNX Runtime shared library (CPU-only on x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, ~50 MB) into the build cache so the test does not require system-installed `libonnxruntime.so` — DEC-0006 spirit (avoid manual operator setup for tests). `default-features = false` on both crates avoids transitive bloat (e.g. `ort`'s `cuda` / `tensorrt` / `directml` execution providers are NOT pulled). The pin to `ort 2.x` is the current stable line as of 2026-Q1; if the executor finds a build break on `2.0`, pick the latest `2.x` patch and document. Add to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`: `ndarray.workspace = true`, `ort.workspace = true`. Existing entries (`thiserror.workspace = true`, `tokio.workspace = true`, `tracing.workspace = true`, `ucil-core = { path = "../ucil-core" }`) MUST keep their alphabetical order; insert the new lines accordingly. AC02 verifies via `grep -E '^ort\.workspace = true' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` and the matching ndarray line.

Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs` (~140-220 LOC including doc + impl + 1 test). Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble cites master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786 verbatim ('ucil-embeddings crate: ONNX Runtime (`ort` crate) inference'). The preamble names: (a) the role of `OnnxSession` as the foundational session abstraction shared across all W8 features (CodeRankEmbed F02, Qwen3 F03, chunker F05, throughput bench F06), (b) the deliberate sync API surface (ONNX Runtime is sync by design; future async wrap is `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` per F02), (c) the input/output-names cache so consumers can introspect once without re-querying the session, (d) the deferral of the actual CodeRankEmbed model download to F02 (P2-W8-F02). Include a `# Examples` block on `OnnxSession::from_path` and `OnnxSession::infer` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. **Module-name discipline**: file name MUST be `onnx_inference.rs` to match the frozen `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F01.acceptance_tests[0].selector = '-p ucil-embeddings onnx_inference::'`. WO-0054 used `ort_session.rs` (mismatched the frozen selector); this WO corrects it.

Declare `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum OnnxSessionError` with variants: (a) `#[error("ort session error: {source}")] Ort { #[from] source: ort::Error }` (auto-conversion from ort errors via `?`); (b) `#[error("ndarray shape error: {source}")] Ndarray { #[from] source: ndarray::ShapeError }`; (c) `#[error("io error reading model file: {source}")] Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }`; (d) `#[error("model has no input named {name:?}")] MissingInput { name: String }`; (e) `#[error("model has no output named {name:?}")] MissingOutput { name: String }`. The `#[non_exhaustive]` attribute follows `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` libraries-use-thiserror invariant.

Declare `pub struct OnnxSession { session: ort::Session, input_names: Vec<String>, output_names: Vec<String> }` with `#[derive(Debug)]` only (NOT `Clone` — `ort::Session` is not `Clone`; consumers that need shared ownership must wrap in `Arc`). The struct is NOT `Send + Sync` automatically — `ort::Session` may or may not be (depends on backend); document the actual auto-trait status in the rustdoc and add `// SAFETY:` notes only if any unsafe trait impl is needed. Most likely `ort::Session: Send + Sync` directly so no manual impl required.

Implement `pub fn from_path(model_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, OnnxSessionError>` with this exact contract: (1) `let session = ort::Session::builder()?.commit_from_file(model_path)?;` (returns `OnnxSessionError::Ort` on parse / file I/O via the `?` chain — `ort::Error` covers both); (2) `let input_names: Vec<String> = session.inputs.iter().map(|input| input.name.clone()).collect();` (3) `let output_names: Vec<String> = session.outputs.iter().map(|output| output.name.clone()).collect();` (4) `Ok(Self { session, input_names, output_names })`. The function is sync — `ort::Session::builder()` and `commit_from_file` are sync. Tracing: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.onnx_session.load", level = "debug", skip(model_path), fields(model_path = ?model_path))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>`. The `model_path: &Path` (NOT `&PathBuf`) follows Rust idiom; callers can pass `&path_buf`.

Implement `pub fn input_names(&self) -> &[String]` and `pub fn output_names(&self) -> &[String]` (zero-cost slice accessors). Rustdoc cites the cache rationale (one tensor introspection at load time → consumers iterate without re-querying).

Implement `pub fn infer(&self, token_ids: &[i64]) -> Result<Vec<f32>, OnnxSessionError>` with this contract: (1) Build a 2-D `ndarray::Array2<i64>` of shape `[1, token_ids.len()]` from the input slice via `ndarray::Array2::from_shape_vec((1, token_ids.len()), token_ids.to_vec())?` (returns `OnnxSessionError::Ndarray` on shape mismatch — defensive; the literal shape construction always matches but the `from_shape_vec` API is fallible). (2) Locate the first input name: `let input_name = self.input_names.first().ok_or_else(|| OnnxSessionError::MissingInput { name: String::from("<first>") })?;` (3) Build the `ort::Value` input via `ort::inputs!{ input_name.as_str() => array.view() }?` (the `inputs!` macro is the ergonomic ort 2.x API; if a future `ort` revision changes the macro name, the executor adapts and documents). (4) `let outputs = self.session.run(inputs)?;` — synchronous run. (5) Locate the first output name: `let output_name = self.output_names.first().ok_or_else(|| OnnxSessionError::MissingOutput { name: String::from("<first>") })?;` (6) Extract `let tensor = outputs[output_name.as_str()].try_extract_tensor::<f32>()?;` (returns `OnnxSessionError::Ort` on dtype mismatch). (7) `let view = tensor.view();` `let flat: Vec<f32> = view.iter().copied().collect();` (8) `Ok(flat)`. The function is sync. Tracing: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.onnx_session.infer", level = "debug", skip(self, token_ids), fields(token_count = token_ids.len()))]`. NOTE: `ort` 2.x specifics may shift the exact API surface; the executor adapts the call shape (e.g. `ort::Value::from_array` vs `ort::inputs!`) while preserving the contract — IF the API has changed materially since the WO was written, document the divergence in the ready-for-review note and NOT in an ADR (no novel design choice — purely fitting upstream).

Add `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/minimal.onnx` — a tiny synthetic ONNX model (~200-2000 bytes) whose graph satisfies the test contract: input `input_ids` of dtype `int64` and shape `[batch=1, seq=*]` (dynamic seq dimension), output `embedding` of dtype `float32` with a deterministic shape that lets `infer(&[1i64, 2, 3])` produce a non-empty `Vec<f32>`. The model body MAY be a single `Cast` op (int64 → float32) or `Identity` over a literal initializer, OR a `Gather` from a tiny embedding table (closer to real CodeRankEmbed structure). The simplest valid graph is `Cast int64 → float32` followed by no-op shape reshape. The executor MAY generate this via `python3 -c 'import onnx, onnx.helper as h; ...; onnx.save(model, "crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/minimal.onnx")'` and check the resulting bytes (along with the generation script at `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/build_minimal_onnx.py` for reproducibility) — OR copy a public-domain tiny ONNX from upstream sources with explicit attribution in a sibling LICENSE.txt. The fixture path `crates/ucil-embeddings/tests/data/` is intentionally inside the crate (NOT under repo-root `tests/fixtures/**` which is forbidden) so the per-crate test discipline holds.

Add the frozen acceptance test `#[test] fn test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The test selector `onnx_inference::test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model` matches the frozen `-p ucil-embeddings onnx_inference::` substring per `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F01.acceptance_tests[0].selector`. Test body (4 sub-assertions in order):  
  **Setup**: locate the bundled fixture via `let manifest_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")); let model_path = manifest_dir.join("tests").join("data").join("minimal.onnx");`. `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` resolves to `crates/ucil-embeddings/` so the fixture lookup is workspace-position-invariant.  
  **(SA1) Session loads**: `let session = OnnxSession::from_path(&model_path).expect("OnnxSession::from_path on minimal model");` — any `Err` panics with the underlying `OnnxSessionError` Debug-print so the verifier sees the actual failure mode.  
  **(SA2) Input metadata accessible**: `let inputs = session.input_names(); assert!(!inputs.is_empty(), "minimal model must declare at least one input; got {:?}", inputs); assert_eq!(inputs[0], "input_ids", "first input name must match the minimal model contract; got {:?}", inputs[0]);` (the executor pins the literal input name in the .onnx fixture so the assertion is deterministic).  
  **(SA3) Output metadata accessible**: `let outputs = session.output_names(); assert!(!outputs.is_empty(), "minimal model must declare at least one output; got {:?}", outputs);` (output name is more variable; just check non-empty).  
  **(SA4) Infer round-trip**: `let result = session.infer(&[1i64, 2, 3]).expect("infer on minimal model"); assert!(!result.is_empty(), "infer must produce a non-empty Vec<f32>; got len={}", result.len()); assert_eq!(result.len(), 3, "minimal model with Cast/Identity over [1,3] returns 3 floats; got len={}", result.len());` — the literal `3` matches the input slice length for an Identity / Cast minimal model. If the executor picks a different minimal model topology (e.g. Gather producing a fixed-dim embedding), the assertion's expected length matches the actual output shape; document any non-`len()==3` choice in the ready-for-review note.  
  All assertions follow the `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", actual)` shape per WO-0042 lessons line 92 + WO-0051 lessons line 405 + WO-0055 lessons line 440.

Update `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs`: (a) add `pub mod onnx_inference;` immediately after the existing crate-level lints block (lines 5-7) so the module is exported; (b) add a NEW re-export line `pub use onnx_inference::{OnnxSession, OnnxSessionError};` immediately after the `pub mod` line. (c) extend the existing module-level `//!` preamble (line 1) with one trailing paragraph describing the `OnnxSession` surface + the F02/F05 deferral pattern (consumers of `OnnxSession` land in subsequent WOs) + master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786 citation. Verify post-edit via `grep -nE 'pub mod onnx_inference;|pub use onnx_inference::' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs | wc -l` returning `2`. Cumulative re-export-discipline streak: 12 consecutive WOs cleared after WO-0056 (WO-0042 → WO-0056). This WO maintains the streak.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` per the standing convention (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F03.sh` post-WO-0056). Script must: (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings onnx_inference::test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model -- --nocapture` and tee stdout to `/tmp/test-WO-0058.log`; (b) match either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests passed` shape via the tolerant alternation `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'` (per WO-0042 / WO-0048 / WO-0051 / WO-0055 / WO-0056 lessons — same reusable shape); (c) on non-zero exit, emit `[FAIL] P2-W8-F01: <reason>` with a hint pointing at `tests/data/minimal.onnx` + the WO + master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1786; (d) `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` so the verifier can run it directly. Header comment block cites WO-0058 + P2-W8-F01 + the test selector verbatim. Include the `shellcheck` PATH-fallback pattern from WO-0044 onwards (`if command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then shellcheck "$0"; else echo "[INFO] shellcheck not on PATH; skipping shellcheck step."; fi`).

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 + WO-0056 AC17 — for `from_path` mutation (M1: early-return Err), the body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables)] { let _ = model_path; return Err(OnnxSessionError::MissingInput { name: String::new() }); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder; for `infer` mutation (M2: empty Vec return), body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables)] { let _ = self; let _ = token_ids; return Ok(Vec::new()); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder. Document BOTH the literal sed AND runtime-only variants in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC15 / AC16).

**Pre-flight rustdoc grep before pushing** (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`): `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs` — every uppercase identifier in rustdoc paragraphs MUST be wrapped in backticks (`` `OnnxSession` ``, `` `ONNX` ``, `` `CodeRankEmbed` ``, etc.). The crate sets `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`; use plain backticks NOT shorthand `[Foo]` intra-doc links unless fully-qualified.

**Operator-readable assertion messages** (per WO-0051 lessons line 405 + WO-0055 line 440): every `assert_eq!` / `assert!` in `test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model` follows the shape `assert!(<predicate>, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", <actual_value>)` so a verifier failure prints the actual observed value, not just the line number.

**Workspace-build precondition** (per WO-0055 line 456 + WO-0056 line 513 standing rule): the executor MUST run `cargo build --workspace --tests` to exit 0 BEFORE writing the ready-for-review marker. Per-crate `cargo check -p ucil-embeddings` is NOT sufficient when adding workspace deps because lib.rs re-export changes (and Cargo.lock churn) are visible to every downstream crate.

**Commit ladder — 6 commits** per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` and DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out:  
  (1) `build(embeddings): add ort + ndarray workspace deps for ONNX Runtime inference` (~15 LOC: 2 workspace deps + 2 `[dependencies]` lines), 
  (2) `feat(embeddings): add OnnxSession skeleton with error type and metadata cache` (~50 LOC: module preamble, `OnnxSessionError`, `OnnxSession` struct, `input_names` + `output_names` accessors), 
  (3) `feat(embeddings): implement OnnxSession::from_path loading via ort::Session::commit_from_file` (~40 LOC: `from_path` + tracing instrument), 
  (4) `feat(embeddings): implement OnnxSession::infer with single-batch ndarray tensor wrapping` (~50 LOC: `infer` + tracing instrument), 
  (5) `test(embeddings): add onnx_inference::test_onnx_session_loads_minimal_model with bundled minimal.onnx fixture` (~80 LOC: minimal.onnx fixture + build_minimal_onnx.py generator + test body — DEC-0005 carve-out: 4 sub-assertions form one coherent regression-guard for the feature contract), 
  (6) `feat(embeddings): wire onnx_inference into lib.rs preamble and re-exports + add scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` (~30 LOC). 
  Each commit signed via the conventional `Phase: 2 / Feature: P2-W8-F01 / Work-order: WO-0058` trailer. If commit-#5 stretches to ~150 LOC due to fixture-generation script size, cite DEC-0005 + WO-0048's 297-LOC framing precedent + WO-0056's 542-LOC NEW-module precedent inline in the commit body per WO-0056 lessons line 502.
scope_out: **No CodeRankEmbed model download or integration.** Master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787 names CodeRankEmbed as the default 137M / CPU embedding model — that is the F02 (P2-W8-F02) deliverable. F01 ships the foundational `OnnxSession` API + a tiny synthetic minimal.onnx fixture for testing only. NO download to `ml/models/coderank/`, NO download to anywhere else; F02 owns the production model placement.

**No Qwen3-Embedding GPU upgrade path.** P2-W8-F03 is the Qwen3 + GPU + Matryoshka-dimension feature; F01 stays CPU-only (the `ort` `cuda` / `tensorrt` / `directml` features are explicitly NOT enabled in the workspace dep). F03's WO will add `cuda` feature gating.

**No async API surface.** `ort::Session::run` is sync; F01 exposes the sync API directly (`OnnxSession::from_path` and `OnnxSession::infer` are `fn`, not `async fn`). The natural async wrap is `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || session.infer(&token_ids))` at the consumer site — that pattern lands in F02 / F05 where the tokio runtime is in play. F01's `OnnxSession` MUST stay sync so the test runs in `#[test]` (NOT `#[tokio::test]`); confirms the API surface is testable without a runtime.

**No tokenizer integration.** The infer() signature accepts pre-tokenized `&[i64]` token IDs; producing those token IDs from raw source text (via `tokenizers` crate or similar) is the F02 / F05 chunker layer. F01 trusts callers to provide the IDs.

**No LanceDB integration.** P2-W8-F04 lands the LanceDB chunk indexer that consumes `OnnxSession::infer` outputs; F01 does NOT wire any database path. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` (WO-0053) is the LanceDB owner — F01 does NOT touch it.

**No interaction with WO-0057.** WO-0057 (in flight; P2-W7-F06 `search_code` G2 fused) modifies only `crates/ucil-daemon/{server.rs, plugin_manager.rs, lib.rs, g2_search.rs (NEW)}` per its AC24 allow-list. WO-0058's allow-list is disjoint (`crates/ucil-embeddings/**` only). The two WOs are safe to run in parallel; merge order does not matter (no shared file edits, no shared test selector, no shared workspace dep — `Cargo.toml` is in WO-0057's `forbidden_paths` per WO-0057:138 so only WO-0058 modifies it).

**No new ADR.** DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC for the test commit specifically), DEC-0006 (download-binaries feature precedent — avoiding manual operator setup for tests) all cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. `ort` 2.x's `inputs!` macro is renamed in the pinned version, or ndarray needs a different shape constructor), STOP and write an ADR before working around. The most likely escalation is upstream ort API drift since ort 2.x is younger than 1.x; document any divergence inline in the rustdoc + ready-for-review marker.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` (M1, M2 per scope_in[12]) are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048 / 0050-superseded / 0051 / 0052 / 0053 / 0055 / 0056, now 12 consecutive cleared excluding the abandoned WO-0054).

**No changes to `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`, `tests/integration/**`, `tests/fixtures/**`** — F01 is single-crate (`ucil-embeddings`), single-source-file (NEW `onnx_inference.rs`) plus the `lib.rs` re-exports + the minimal.onnx fixture under the crate's own `tests/data/` (NOT repo-root `tests/fixtures/**`).
**No `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

**No mock of `ort::Session`, `ort::SessionBuilder`, `ort::Value`, or `ndarray::Array`.** ONNX Runtime is a critical dep per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` (no mocks of Serena, LSP, SQLite, LanceDB, Docker, ONNX Runtime); the test uses real `ort::Session::commit_from_file` against a real on-disk minimal.onnx fixture. WO-0046 lessons line 248 confirms tempfile-/fixture-backed real-binary patterns are the correct approach.
**No `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
acceptance: AC01: `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` declares `ort` and `ndarray` — verified via `grep -cE '^ort = |^ndarray = ' Cargo.toml` returning `2`.
